text: Increasing microplastic contamination in daily food items poses significant health risks. Current detection methods are time-consuming, expensive, and require lab infrastructure.

{"target_customers": ["Food brands", "Food safety regulators", "Food safety testing labs", "Research labs", "Health-conscious individuals", "Parents", "Environmentally aware people"], "customer_segments": ["Research Labs & Industrial Users", "Regulatory agencies", "Food & Beverage companies", "Academia & Food safety companies", "Packaged Water Manufacturers labs"], "market_size": {"TAM": "$5B+ (Global microplastic detection & food safety market)", "SAM": "~$1.2B (Food & beverage, packaged water, seafood industry testing)", "SOM": "~$100M (Early adopters in regulatory agencies & premium food manufacturers)"}, "market_drivers": ["Food safety is a priority in high-impact industries", "Regulatory compliance pressure requiring cost-effective and portable solutions", "Scalability with potential recurring revenue"], "competition": "Current methods are lab-based, time-consuming, and expensive; competitors not specified in detail", "validation": "Customer discovery through interviews with senior scientists and professors in biotechnology"}

{"prototype_steps": ["Load the sample", "Enzymatic degradation and size-based separation using microfluidics (lab on a chip)", "Detection of microplastics using SERS technology", "Identification and quantification of microplastics using AI"], "technology": ["Surface Enhanced Raman Spectroscopy (SERS)", "Microfluidic chip for enzymatic degradation and size-based separation", "Artificial Intelligence for identification and quantification"]}

Portable microplastic detector offering real-time detection with high accuracy and specificity, combining enzymatic degradation, microfluidics, SERS detection, and AI analysis, which is faster, less expensive, and does not require lab infrastructure compared to existing methods.

{"health": "Reduces health risks by enabling quick detection of microplastic contamination in food.", "regulatory": "Helps food brands and regulatory agencies comply with stricter food safety regulations.", "market": "Provides a desirable, simple, inexpensive, and accurate alternative to lab-based testing, benefiting brands, labs, and regulatory bodies."}

Protect households from false data injection attacks (FDIA) in smart grids by providing an accessible, real-time anomaly detection system to prevent inflated bills, device damage, and privacy breaches.

{"market_size": "India has over 90 million smart meters installed as of 2024; smart home market estimated at USD 7.3B by 2025.", "target_segments": ["Tech-savvy urban households with high-speed internet and multiple smart devices", "Small businesses with sensitive equipment"], "customer_needs": ["Protection against FDIA and voltage fluctuations", "Willingness to pay 10-15% premium for advanced appliance protection", "Preference for simplified dashboards and plug-and-play installation"], "market_opportunity": {"cybersecurity_opportunity": "\u20b93,500Cr (~$420M) by 2025 targeting 250M smart meters", "regulatory_drivers": "National Cybersecurity Strategy 2023 and BIS IS 17855:2022 mandate grid protection", "cyberattack_trends": "61% YoY increase in power sector cyberattacks; FDIA attempts up 290% globally", "competitive_gap": "Enterprise solutions cost \u20b915L+; general cybersecurity lacks FDIA focus; no existing consumer-level FDIA protection"}, "growth": "Consumer cybersecurity market CAGR ~19.4%, current penetration <5%; rising renewable integration (175 GW by 2025)"}

{"solution_description": "A device integrating smart voltage regulation with AI-powered FDIA detection using precision sensors and a deep LSTM neural network to monitor household voltage and current in real time.", "architecture": {"edge_monitoring": "Real-time sensor data acquisition", "local_analytics": "LSTM-based engine (3 layers, 64 units each) comparing expected vs actual measurements using MSE analysis", "distributed_intelligence": "Optional network of devices sharing anonymized threat data to refine detection accuracy"}, "hardware_components": ["Buck/Boost Converter", "ESP32 Microcontroller", "SD Card", "Solid State Relay (SSR)", "ESP8266 Wi-Fi Module", "ACS712 Current Sensor", "ZMPT101B Voltage Sensor"], "software_stack": ["Microcontroller Firmware", "Communication & Cloud Integration", "Data Processing & ML Model Development", "Visualization & User Interface", "Security & Communication Protocols"], "validation": "Prototype validated on UCI Household Electric Power Consumption dataset and testbed simulations achieving ~92% detection accuracy with <3% false positives"}

First consumer-level solution merging voltage stabilization with AI-driven FDIA detection, using a three-tier architecture combining edge monitoring, local LSTM analytics, and distributed intelligence for proactive anomaly detection beyond traditional voltage stabilizers.
